evidence: WordSketchApiServer.java (~700 lines, 15+ HTTP handler methods, the primary user-facing API layer) has zero test coverage — no unit tests, no integration tests, no contract tests for any of its endpoints
SketchHandlers.java (a second handler class with 9+ handler methods) also has zero tests

BlackLabQueryExecutor.java (~540 lines, the core query engine that all API results flow through) has no direct unit tests; BlackLabBcqlConcordanceTest and BlackLabDummyTest exist but are integration tests that require a real BlackLab index
BlackLabConllUIndexer.java (the indexer) has no unit tests at all
RadialPlot.java (SVG generator used by /api/visual/radial) has no dedicated test — viz/ test dir is empty

Test coverage is present for: LongIntHashMap, LogDiceCalculator, BlackLabSnippetParser (328 lines of tests), SemanticFieldExplorer (stub-based, good), PatternSubstitution, SimpleTagger, GrammarConfigLoader/RelationsValidation, HybridAlgorithm (grammar config shapes)
SemanticFieldExplorer tests use a well-designed StubExecutor that isolates the exploration algorithm from BlackLab — this is the right approach but it's the only class that follows it
Integer.parseInt in handleConcordanceExamples (line 813) has no NFE guard and no test exercising invalid input — the API contract for parameter validation is untested

No test for the resolveRelationAlias switch in WordSketchApiServer, which maps public-facing aliases (e.g., 'adj_predicate') to internal IDs — a silent mismatch there would break the API with no test catching it

strengths: SemanticFieldExplorer is tested with a clean StubExecutor pattern that fully decouples the algorithm from the BlackLab index — this is good test design
BlackLabSnippetParser has thorough unit tests (328 lines) covering the XML parsing and boundary trimming logic
LogDiceCalculator and LongIntHashMap both have focused unit tests covering edge cases (zero denominators, rehash behavior)
test_strategy.issue_character: The untested surface is structurally concentrated at the API and query-execution layers — exactly the components with the most complexity and the most user-facing failure modes — while lower-level utilities are well-covered.
test_strategy.score_rationale: Utilities and parsing logic are well tested, and the SemanticFieldExplorer stub approach demonstrates awareness of testable design. However, WordSketchApiServer (the entire HTTP layer), SketchHandlers, BlackLabQueryExecutor (the core engine), and BlackLabConllUIndexer are completely untested. By line count, the untested production code substantially exceeds the tested production code. A codebase at 60 would have at least HTTP endpoint contract tests; at 50 it would have query executor unit tests. This falls below both thresholds.

issues.summary: WordSketchApiServer and SketchHandlers have zero test coverage despite containing ~700 lines of HTTP handler logic including parameter validation, error handling, and JSON serialization
evidence: No test file exists under src/test/java/.../api/ for WordSketchApiServer or SketchHandlers
WordSketchApiServer contains 15+ handler methods including resolveRelationAlias (which maps public aliases to internal IDs) with no tests
Integer.parseInt/Double.parseDouble without NFE guards at lines 708-709, 786, 813 have no test asserting 400 response on invalid input
CORS and OPTIONS preflight logic in registerGetHandler is untested
issues.suggestion: Add unit tests using a mock QueryExecutor and mock HttpExchange (or an embedded HttpServer) to cover at minimum: valid requests return 200, missing required params return 400, invalid numeric params return 400, and relation alias resolution is correct. The StubExecutor pattern from SemanticFieldExplorerTest is directly applicable.

issues.summary: BlackLabQueryExecutor has no unit tests for its core logic (findCollocations, executeSurfacePattern, buildAndRankCollocates), leaving the primary query path untested without a live index
evidence: BlackLabBcqlConcordanceTest and BlackLabDummyTest are integration tests that open a real BlackLab index — they are skipped or fail in environments without the index
buildAndRankCollocates (private helper that applies logDice filtering and sorting) has no dedicated test path exercisable without BlackLab
The collocate extraction logic (extractCollocateFromMatch, extractCollocateLemma fallback chain) in findCollocations is only reachable through a live index
issues.suggestion: Extract buildAndRankCollocates to a package-private or protected scope and add a unit test that exercises it with a pre-built frequency map, a mock getTotalFrequency, and specific minLogDice thresholds. The collocate extraction can be tested via the existing BlackLabSnippetParser unit tests.

issues.summary: RadialPlot SVG generator has no tests despite having non-trivial spiral layout math and XML escaping logic
evidence: src/test/java/.../viz/ directory exists but is empty — no test class for RadialPlot
RadialPlot.toSVG() computes spiral positions, scales constants by canvas size, escapes XML, and has a signed-mode color branch — all untested paths
escapeXml: replaces &, <, >, ", ' — incorrect escaping would produce invalid SVG silently
issues.suggestion: Add a RadialPlotTest that: (1) asserts toSVG() produces valid XML for a known item list, (2) asserts escapeXml handles special characters, (3) asserts signed-mode generates different fill colors than unsigned mode.

root_causes: API and query layers were developed against a live BlackLab index, making unit testing feel blocked on infrastructure — the SemanticFieldExplorer stub pattern was not applied to other layers
Test files were added incrementally for utilities as they were created, but the HTTP and executor layers were never revisited for testability
